task_completion.description: The 'Suspect_Vehicle' and 'Refuge_Boundary' tracks are visible on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map interface shows the 'Suspect_Vehicle' and 'Refuge_Boundary' tracks loaded in the layers/places panel and visible on the map.

task_completion.description: The agent locates the intersection of the two tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is centered or zoomed in on the exact point where the 'Suspect_Vehicle' and 'Refuge_Boundary' tracks cross each other.

task_completion.description: A waypoint is created at the intersection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new waypoint marker is placed exactly at the intersection of the two tracks on the map.
The properties dialog for the waypoint or the layers/places panel displays the name 'Incursion' for the newly created waypoint.

task_completion.description: The waypoint is exported to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or export dialog is visible showing the save path 'C:\workspace\output\incursion_point.gpx', or a file explorer confirms the file's creation in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the mapping software's graphical interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with map tools, menus, and dialog boxes rather than typing terminal commands to generate the output.

integrity.description: The export was performed through the application's native export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of the mapping application's 'Save As' or export dialog rather than a script writing the file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write a GPX file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like Notepad or VS Code) are open with manually typed GPX XML content.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is 'C:\workspace\output\incursion_point.gpx'. The expected waypoint name is 'Incursion'. The spatial accuracy thresholds are 30.0 meters (excellent) and 100.0 meters (fair).
